factors.summary: Overall demand is decelerating, particularly in on-highway truck markets.

evidence.text: Worldwide revenues decreased by 3 percent in the three months ended March 31, 2025, compared to the same period in 2024, primarily due to the divestiture of Atmus and weaker demand in on-highway truck markets.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Demand is decelerating in key markets, indicating a potential slowdown.

sentiment.label: neutral

factors.summary: The company has pricing power, as gross margin improved due to favorable pricing.

evidence.text: Gross margin increased $114 million for the three months ended March 31, 2025, and increased 2.1 points as a percentage of net sales versus the comparable period in 2024. The increases in gross margin and gross margin as a percentage of sales were primarily due to favorable pricing and lower material costs.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Improved gross margins suggest strong pricing power.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments, particularly power generation markets.

evidence.text: The decrease in net income attributable to Cummins Inc. and earnings per diluted share were driven by the absence of the $1.3 billion gain recognized on the divestiture of Atmus, partially offset by improved gross margin. The increase in gross margin was primarily due to favorable pricing and lower material costs, partially offset by the absence of Atmus sales, lower sales and unfavorable mix.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Shift towards higher-margin segments indicates a strategic positive move.

factors.summary: Power generation markets are the fastest-growing end market, while on-highway truck markets are slowing.

evidence.text: The decrease in net income attributable to Cummins Inc. and earnings per diluted share were driven by the absence of the $1.3 billion gain recognized on the divestiture of Atmus, partially offset by improved gross margin. The increase in gross margin was primarily due to favorable pricing and lower material costs, partially offset by the absence of Atmus sales, lower sales and unfavorable mix.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: On-highway truck markets are slowing, despite growth in power generation.

factors.summary: Product quality incidents are a risk, with potential recalls noted.

evidence.text: • product recalls;

factors.summary: Regulatory changes, particularly environmental and climate change legislation, are creating compliance costs and operational constraints.

evidence.text: • evolving environmental and climate change legislation and regulatory initiatives;

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Regulatory changes are leading to compliance costs and operational issues.

factors.summary: Environmental regulations are a significant compliance cost and operational constraint.

evidence.text: • increased scrutiny from regulatory agencies, as well as unpredictability in the adoption, implementation and enforcement of emission standards around the world;

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Increased regulatory scrutiny adds compliance costs and operational constraints.

factors.summary: Trade tariffs are negatively affecting the company's supply chain and potentially future results.

evidence.text: The escalating tariff environment... has introduced significant market volatility and raised concerns about potential economic impacts. This volatility accelerated late in the first quarter of 2025. We are proactively taking steps in our supply chain to mitigate impacts where possible and we are working with our customers to pass through incremental costs.

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: The evidence indicates significant negative impacts on the supply chain and potential future results due to tariffs.

factors.summary: Litigation exposure exists from regulatory settlements, including potential legal actions and reputational impacts.

evidence.text: • any adverse consequences resulting from entering into agreements with the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency, California Air Resources Board, the Environmental and Natural Resources Division of the Department of Justice and the California Attorney General's Office to resolve certain regulatory civil claims regarding our emissions certification and compliance process for certain engines primarily used in pick-up truck applications in the U.S., which became final and effective in April 2024, (collectively, the Settlement Agreements), including required additional mitigation projects, adverse reputational impacts and potential resulting legal actions;

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: Litigation exposure from regulatory settlements poses risks including legal actions and reputational damage.

factors.summary: Forward guidance indicates potential future impacts from tariffs and other risks, but specific numbers are not provided.

evidence.text: See the 'OUTLOOK' section for a discussion of the potential tariff impacts for the remainder of 2025.

sentiment.label: negative

factors.summary: Management is cautious about future impacts from tariffs and trade disruptions but is taking proactive steps to mitigate risks.

evidence.text: We are proactively taking steps in our supply chain to mitigate impacts where possible and we are working with our customers to pass through incremental costs. This escalation in market volatility did not have a material impact on our first quarter results, but may materially and adversely affect our results of operations, financial condition and cash flows in the future. We will continue work to minimize the related impacts to our business to the extent possible.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Management is cautious but taking steps to mitigate risks, indicating neither clear positive nor negative sentiment.

factors.summary: The company's competitive advantages include technology leadership and a broad product portfolio.

evidence.text: Our products range from advanced diesel, natural gas, electric and hybrid powertrains and powertrain-related components including aftertreatment, turbochargers, fuel systems, valvetrain technologies, controls systems, air handling systems, automated transmissions, axles, drivelines, brakes, suspension systems, electric power generation systems, electrified power systems with innovative components and subsystems, including battery, fuel cell and electric power technologies and hydrogen production technologies.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company has a competitive advantage through technology leadership and a broad product portfolio, which is a positive sentiment.

factors.summary: Customer concentration is managed through a diverse customer base, including long-standing relationships with major OEMs.

evidence.text: We serve our customers through a service network of approximately 650 wholly-owned, joint venture and independent distributor locations and more than 19,000 Cummins certified dealer locations in approximately 190 countries and territories. We have long-standing relationships with many of the leading manufacturers in the markets we serve, including PACCAR Inc., Traton Group, Daimler Trucks North America and Stellantis N.V.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: The evidence shows a diverse customer base with long-standing relationships, but there's no indication of concentration risk.

factors.summary: The company is focused on maintaining strong cash flow and considering buybacks and dividends, while also maintaining its dividend payments, indicating confidence in its financial position.

evidence.text: We paid dividends of $251 million during the three months ended March 31, 2025.
We used $3 million of cash in operations for the three months ended March 31, 2025, compared to generating $276 million for the comparable period in 2024.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company is maintaining strong cash flow and considering buybacks and dividends, showing confidence in its financial position.

factors.summary: The company is planning to increase capital expenditures, primarily in North America, to support growth and innovation. While specific figures are not provided in the Overview subsection, the Dividends/Capital Expenditures section indicates a significant investment of $1.4 billion to $1.5 billion in 2025. The Research, Development, and Engineering Expenses subsection highlights investments in new product development and digital technologies, which align with the capital expenditure plans.

evidence.text: We plan to spend an estimated $1.4 billion to $1.5 billion in 2025 on capital expenditures with over 65 percent of these expenditures expected to be invested in North America.

Research activities continue to focus on development of new products and improvements of current technologies to meet future emission standards around the world, improvements in fuel economy performance of diesel and natural gas-powered engines and related components, as well as development activities around electrified power systems with innovative components and systems including battery and electric power technologies and hydrogen production technologies.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company is planning significant capital expenditures to support growth and innovation, indicating a proactive approach to expansion.

factors.summary: The company's debt leverage remains stable, with a slight increase in the debt to capital ratio, reflecting a balanced approach to capital structure management. The stable credit ratings and strong financial condition support the company's access to credit and capital markets.

evidence.text: Our financial condition and liquidity remain strong. Our solid balance sheet and credit ratings enable us to have ready access to credit and the capital markets.
Total debt as a percent of total capital was 38.5% at March 31, 2025, compared to 38.4% at December 31, 2024.
evidence.subsection: Key Working Capital and Balance Sheet Data

evidence.text: Our debt to capital ratio (total capital defined as debt plus equity) at March 31, 2025, was 38.5 percent, compared to 38.4 percent at December 31, 2024. The increase was primarily due to higher debt balances at March 31, 2025, partially offset by increased equity balances from strong earnings since December 31, 2024.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Debt leverage is stable with a slight increase, reflecting a balanced approach without clear positive or negative trends.

factors.summary: The company's free cash flow situation appears mixed, with some evidence indicating stable generation but others pointing to deteriorating performance. While one subsection highlights stable cash flow and specific uses of cash, another notes a significant increase in operating cash outflows and a net decrease in cash and cash equivalents, suggesting potential challenges in maintaining financial flexibility.

evidence.text: We believe our access to the capital markets, our existing cash and marketable securities, operating cash flow and revolving credit facilities provide us with the financial flexibility needed to fund targeted capital expenditures, dividend payments, debt service obligations, projected pension obligations, common stock repurchases, joint venture contributions and acquisitions through 2025 and beyond.

Net cash used in operating activities increased $279 million for the three months ended March 31, 2025, versus the comparable period in 2024, primarily due to higher working capital requirements of $375 million.
We used $3 million of cash in operations for the three months ended March 31, 2025, compared to generating $276 million for the comparable period in 2024.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed signals about cash flow stability, with some evidence of challenges in maintaining financial flexibility.

factors.summary: The company has demonstrated active management and strategic investments through significant contributions to its joint venture and successful divestitures, which have generated substantial financial gains.

evidence.text: As of March 31, 2025, we contributed $255 million to our Amplify Cell Technologies LLC joint venture and our maximum remaining required contribution was $551 million, which could be reduced by future government incentives received by the joint venture. The majority of the contribution is expected to be made by the end of 2028.
evidence.subsection: Amplify Cell Technologies LLC Joint Venture

evidence.text: On March 18, 2024, we completed the divestiture of our remaining 80.5 percent ownership of Atmus Filtration Technologies Inc. (Atmus) common stock through a tax-free split-off. The exchange resulted in a reduction of shares of our common stock outstanding by 5.6 million shares and a gain of $1.3 billion.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Active management and strategic investments, including joint ventures and successful divestitures, have generated significant gains.

factors.summary: Input costs are stable or declining, contributing to improved gross margins, and the company is managing them effectively through favorable pricing and productivity improvements. While there are some offsetting factors like lower volumes and unfavorable mix, the overall impact on EBITDA and gross margins has been positive.

evidence.text: "Engine segment EBITDA for the three months ended March 31, 2025, increased $44 million versus the comparable period in 2024, primarily due to favorable pricing related to the launch of updated products in light-duty markets, partially offset by lower volumes and unfavorable mix."

"The increases in gross margin and gross margin as a percentage of sales were primarily due to favorable pricing and lower material costs, partially offset by the absence of Atmus sales, lower sales and unfavorable mix."

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Stable or declining input costs, along with effective management, have contributed to improved gross margins.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are declining across multiple segments, including the Engine and Components segments. The decline in the Engine segment is evident across various product lines, such as heavy-duty, medium-duty, and light-duty units. Additionally, the Components segment experienced a 20% decrease in sales, primarily due to the Atmus divestiture.

evidence.text: Unit shipments by engine classification (including unit shipments to Power Systems and off-highway engine units included in their respective classification) were as follows: Three months ended March 31, 2025, showed a decrease in heavy-duty, medium-duty, and light-duty unit shipments compared to 2024.
Components segment sales decreased 20 percent mainly due to the Atmus divestiture on March 18, 2024.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Declining unit volumes across segments suggest a potential downturn in demand or market conditions.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is decelerating, with significant declines in the Engine and Components segments, particularly due to weaker demand in heavy-duty truck sales. The decrease is also influenced by the divestiture of Atmus and a broader reduction in on-highway truck market demand.

evidence.text: Engine segment sales for the three months ended March 31, 2025, decreased $157 million versus the comparable period in 2024. The primary driver by market was a decrease in heavy-duty truck sales of $138 million mainly due to weaker demand, especially in North America with heavy-duty engine shipments down 24 percent.

Worldwide revenues decreased by 3 percent in the three months ended March 31, 2025, compared to the same period in 2024, primarily due to the divestiture of Atmus and weaker demand in on-highway truck markets.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The factor shows decelerating organic growth with significant declines in key segments and weaker demand.

factors.summary: The impact of labor costs on margins appears to be mixed. While labor costs are creating margin pressure due to the divestiture of Atmus, they are also noted to be manageable and not a significant factor in margin pressure.

evidence.text: The decreases [in Selling, general and administrative expenses] were mainly due to lower compensation and consulting expenses, primarily due to the divestiture of Atmus. Compensation and related expenses included salaries, fringe benefits and variable compensation.
No specific mention of labor cost pressures or wage inflation impacting margins.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / ITEM 2.  Management’s Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations / CAUTIONARY STATEMENTS REGARDING FORWARD-LOOKING INFORMATION

sentiment.label: neutral

factors.summary: The company has implemented cost reduction actions, primarily through the divestiture of Atmus, which have resulted in measurable savings. evidence.text: The decreases [in Research, development and engineering expenses] were mainly due to lower compensation expenses, primarily due to the divestiture of Atmus.
We are proactively taking steps in our supply chain to mitigate impacts where possible and we are working with our customers to pass through incremental costs.

sentiment.label: positive

factors.summary: Productivity improvements are evident, particularly in segments where operational efficiency has increased. Productivity is improving, contributing to higher gross margins.

evidence.text: Components segment EBITDA for the three months ended March 31, 2025, decreased $91 million versus the comparable period in 2024, mainly due to lower volumes and the divestiture of Atmus, partially offset by decreased product coverage costs and lower material costs.

The increases in gross margin and gross margin as a percentage of sales were primarily due to favorable pricing and lower material costs, partially offset by the absence of Atmus sales, lower sales and unfavorable mix.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Productivity improvements are evident, contributing to higher gross margins.

factors.summary: The company's margin trajectory shows mixed performance across different segments. While gross and operating margins are expanding in certain areas, such as the Engine and Components segments, other segments like Power Systems are experiencing compressions. Overall, the trend is mixed with some positive developments and some areas needing attention.

evidence.text: Engine segment EBITDA as a percentage of total sales increased from 14.1% to 16.5%. However, Components segment EBITDA as a percentage of total sales remained stable at 14.2%, while Power Systems segment EBITDA margin expanded significantly.

Gross margin increased $114 million for the three months ended March 31, 2025, and increased 2.1 points as a percentage of net sales versus the comparable period in 2024. Operating income increased by 211 million, or 23 percent, compared to the prior year period.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed performance across segments with some expansion and some compression.

factors.summary: The company's R&D investment is showing a mixed trend, with a decrease in spending due to the divestiture of Atmus, but ongoing efforts in new product development, particularly in electrified power systems.

evidence.text: Research, development and engineering expenses decreased $25 million for the three months ended March 31, 2025, and decreased 0.2 points as a percentage of net sales versus the comparable period in 2024. The decreases were mainly due to lower compensation expenses, primarily due to the divestiture of Atmus.

The Accelera segment is currently in the early stages of commercializing these technologies with efforts primarily focused on the development of electrified power systems and related components and subsystems and our electrolyzers for hydrogen production.

factors.summary: Economic conditions are acting as a headwind across various sectors, particularly impacting on-highway truck markets and emerging markets. The increased tariffs are creating uncertainty and reducing demand for capital goods, with broader implications for global trade.

evidence.text: The increase in tariffs has impacted the global movement of goods and introduced uncertainty around future demand for capital goods. Given the breadth, severity and uncertainty about the duration of global tariffs it is not possible to confidently predict market demand for the remainder of this year.

Economic downturns in markets we serve generally result in reduced sales of our products and can result in price reductions in certain products and/or markets. Our sales may also be impacted by OEM inventory levels, production schedules, stoppages and supply chain challenges.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Economic conditions are acting as a headwind with increased tariffs and reduced demand.

factors.summary: The company faces mixed foreign exchange impacts, with favorable translation adjustments contributing to gains, but unfavorable fluctuations negatively affecting international sales.

evidence.text: The foreign currency translation adjustment was a net gain of $117 million for the three months ended March 31, 2025, compared to a net loss of $60 million for the three months ended March 31, 2024, driven by the following: Brazilian real, British pound, Euro translation gains offsetting Chinese renminbi losses.
evidence.subsection: Comprehensive Income - Foreign Currency Translation Adjustment

evidence.text: Unfavorable foreign currency fluctuations impacted international sales by 4 percent (primarily the Brazilian real, Euro and Indian rupee).

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed impacts from foreign exchange gains and losses, with some positive and negative effects.

factors.summary: Geopolitical factors such as increased tariffs, trade tensions, and sanctions are creating significant risks, leading to market demand uncertainty and potential disruptions. The ongoing uncertainties surrounding the duration and impact of these measures make it difficult to predict future demand and stability.

evidence.text: The increase in tariffs has impacted the global movement of goods and introduced uncertainty around future demand for capital goods. Given the breadth, severity and uncertainty about the duration of global tariffs it is not possible to confidently predict market demand for the remainder of this year.
political, economic and social uncertainty and the evolving globalization of our business;

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Significant risks from increased tariffs and trade tensions leading to demand uncertainty.

factors.summary: The company is experiencing a positive impact from interest rate changes, as evidenced by a decrease in interest expense due to lower borrowing costs. Additionally, the company has effectively managed its interest rate exposure through hedging activities, particularly by settling interest rate swaps, which further contributes to its financial stability.

evidence.text: Interest expense was $77 million for the three months ended March 31, 2025, versus $89 million for the comparable period in 2024. The $12 million decrease was primarily due to lower average debt balances and lower weighted-average interest rates.

In the first quarter of 2025, we settled the remaining $350 million of interest rate swaps, at their expiration date, on our debt due in 2025. See NOTE 13, 'DERIVATIVES,' to our Condensed Consolidated Financial Statements for additional information.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Lower interest expenses and effective hedging contributing to financial stability.

factors.summary: Construction activity and infrastructure spending are creating demand for the company's products, though specific details about their impact on the Power Systems segment are limited. evidence.text: Sales for our Power Systems segment by product line were as follows: Power generation sales increased $148 million mainly due to higher demand in China, North America and Western Europe. Industrial sales increased $78 million primarily due to stronger demand in global mining markets (including higher aftermarket sales) and improved defense sales in North America.
Construction activity and infrastructure spending are creating growing demand for the company's products.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / ITEM 2.  Management’s Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations / CAUTIONARY STATEMENTS REGARDING FORWARD-LOOKING INFORMATION

factors.summary: The aftermarket/service revenue is growing, particularly in the Distribution segment, which has seen a 12% increase in segment EBITDA. evidence.text: Distribution segment EBITDA for the three months ended March 31, 2025, increased $82 million versus the comparable period in 2024, primarily due to increased power generation volumes in North America.

The Distribution segment includes wholly-owned and partially-owned distributorships engaged in wholesaling engines, generator sets and service parts, as well as performing service and repair activities on our products, maintaining relationships with various OEMs throughout the world and providing selected sales and aftermarket support for our Accelera business.
